title: Introduction to Digital Identity
learningObjectives: Explain the concept of "context collapse" (also referred to as "collapsed context").
Introduce yourself to the rest of the class using an online discussion board.

title: Philosophical Perspectives on Identity
learningObjectives: Define the term "Identity."
Examine who we are and the influence of digital spaces, tools, and environments on our own human personality.
assessments: Module 2 Assignment: Who am I? Mind Map

title: Identity in Digital Realms
learningObjectives: Analyze your own identity in digital spaces by conducting a thorough self-audit of your online breadcrumbs.
Examine who we are and the influence of digital spaces, tools, and environments on our own human personality.
Define how parent and adult attitudes and perceptions of online spaces differ from those of young adults and children.

title: Identity in a Rapidly Changing World - Part 1
learningObjectives: Review Moore's Law, Fuller's Knowledge Doubling Theory, Kurzweil's Law of Accelerating Returns, and Thiel's "End of the Future" theories.
Appraise the current relevance of theories proposed by Fuller, Moore, Kurzweil, and Thiel's theory "End of the Future."
Consider the recent past and make predictions of what is to come next.
assessments: Module 4 Quiz: Moore, Fuller, Kurzweil, & Thiel

title: Identity in a Rapidly Changing World - Part 2
learningObjectives: Define converging technologies and their impact on all of humanity.
Synthesize the readings and lecture on future technologies into key takeaways.

title: Irresistible: Addictive Technology
learningObjectives: Compare and contrast your social media usage with your classmates.
Explore the connection between behavioral addiction and technology.

title: Social Media - Part 1
learningObjectives: Identify how social media and other digital technologies may be harmful to people.
Create a visual representation of important dates that affect our digital lives.

title: Video Games and Identity
notes: The provided PDF does not list learning objectives for this module.

title: Turning Off (Life Off the Digital Grid)
learningObjectives: Reflect on how the digital world controls our identity and sense of self by disconnecting from it and reflecting on the experience in a written report or video diary.
Examine how pervasive our devices and digital spaces prevail in our lives and desires by stepping away from them.
assessments: Module 9 Assignment: Turning Off (Life Off the Digital Grid)

title: Social Media - Part 2
notes: The provided PDF does not list learning objectives for this module.

title: Personal Data & Data Mining
learningObjectives: Explain how companies, governments, and industries use data for profit and their own gain.
Teach others about digital tracking through digital footprints and digital shadows.

title: The App Generation
learningObjectives: Discuss the role mobile apps play in identity.
Analyze what their cell phone apps say about their personality and identity.
Create a digital artifact for your final project website.
assessments: Module 12 Discussion: What Our Apps Say About Us
Module 12 Assignment: Create an Artifact for your Final Project

title: Emerging Technologies & Identity
learningObjectives: Create a presentation on a chosen emerging technology topic.
Identify and reflect on your own use and relationships with these emerging technologies.
Identify emerging technologies and describe their traits.

title: #GEA Final Website Project
learningObjectives: Analyze a digital identity issue in an in-depth fashion.
Create a website.
Share the website with others.
assessments: Assignment: Create a new digital artifact for your final project website
